question: At what time on 2026-04-28 was the Boil-Water Notice for Service Zone 2 issued?
What is the identifier of the Lift Notice issued on 2026-05-04?
Which Service Zone is covered by BWN-2026-04-28-A?
reference_answer: Service Zone 2.

question: Which Service Zone is covered by RUN-2026-04-28-B?
reference_answer: Service Zone 3.

question: What was the recorded cause of the sampler going offline at event E-04?
reference_answer: Power supply fault.

question: What was the recorded cause of the sampler going offline at event E-08?
reference_answer: Routine sampler maintenance.

question: Who is recorded as the compiler of this packet?
reference_answer: Operations Officer K. Alvarez.

question: Who is named as the Metrology Technician in the packet?
reference_answer: D. Pham.

question: Who is named as the Communications Officer who drafted BWN-2026-04-28-A, RUN-2026-04-28-B, and LFT-2026-05-04?
reference_answer: M. Cortes, Communications Officer.

question: On what date did the metrology technician perform the review of the S-3 sampler-controller clock drift?
What is the corrected timestamp of event E-04 under §6.5?
reference_answer: 2026-04-29 03:45 (one hour earlier than the as-logged 04:45 because the sampler controller clock had drifted one hour ahead; corrected under §6.5).

question: What was the as-logged (uncorrected) timestamp of event E-04?
reference_answer: 2026-04-29 04:45 as logged on the local controller; this timestamp is not operative for rule computations.

question: At what date and time was the §6.1 24-hour clear-sample requirement met?
reference_answer: 2026-05-01 17:00 (Friday). 24 hours of clear-sample time accumulated across two active segments separated by a §6.2a pause from 09:00 to 11:00 on 2026-05-01.

question: At what date and time did the §6.3 48-hour lift-notification clock begin?
reference_answer: 2026-05-01 17:30, when Operations Officer Alvarez logged §6.1 satisfied (event E-11).

question: What would the §6.3 48-hour deadline have been if the weekend-shift rule had not been applied?
What is the §6.3 lift-notification deadline as adjusted under the weekend-shift rule?
reference_answer: 2026-05-04 17:00. The unshifted Sunday deadline shifts to close of business (17:00) on the next business day, Monday 2026-05-04.

question: What was the total active duration of BWN-2026-04-28-A from issuance to lift?
reference_answer: 6 days, 4 hours (from issuance at 2026-04-28 08:00 to lift at 2026-05-04 12:00).

question: What was the total active duration of RUN-2026-04-28-B from issuance to lift?
reference_answer: 6 days, 3 hours, 30 minutes (from issuance at 2026-04-28 09:30 to lift at 2026-05-04 13:00).

question: What was the duration of the first sampler-offline interval (E-04 corrected to E-05)?
reference_answer: 10 hours, 35 minutes (from corrected E-04 time 2026-04-29 03:45 to E-05 at 2026-04-29 14:20).

question: What was the duration of the second sampler-offline interval (E-08 to E-09)?
Over what date-and-time interval was the §6.1 clear-sample clock paused under §6.2a during the event?
reference_answer: 2026-05-01 09:00 to 2026-05-01 11:00 (the E-08 / E-09 maintenance window; the §6.2a pause is the only pause that occurred during the active clear-sample countdown).

question: How many distinct sampler-offline intervals occurred at Station S-3 during the event?
reference_answer: 2 (the power-supply-fault interval starting at corrected 03:45 on 2026-04-29 and the maintenance interval on 2026-05-01).

question: How many positive samples (E. coli) were detected at Station S-3 during the event documented in §2?
How many distinct notices (BWN, RUN, or Lift Notice) were issued during the event documented in §2?
How many event rows are listed in the chronological event log in §2?
reference_answer: 13 (E-01 through E-13).

question: How many open items are listed at packet close in §7?
reference_answer: 3 (engineering review report on contamination cause; procedure review on OPS-PROJ-04-29; and formal filing of the metrology technician's clock-drift report).

question: What was the operational state of sampler S-3 at 2026-04-29 04:30 according to the snapshot table?
reference_answer: Offline (since 03:45 corrected). The first sampler-offline interval was in progress.

question: What was the state of BWN-2026-04-28-A at 2026-05-04 13:00 according to the snapshot table?
reference_answer: Lifted. The BWN was lifted at 2026-05-04 12:00, and 13:00 on the same date is after that lift time.

question: What was the state of the clear-sample clock at 2026-05-01 10:00 according to the snapshot table, and how many hours had been counted?
reference_answer: Paused under §6.2a, with 18 hours counted at that moment.

question: What was the operational state of sampler S-3 at 2026-04-30 12:00 according to the snapshot table?
reference_answer: Operational. The sampler had been restored at 2026-04-29 14:20 and was operational through 2026-05-01 09:00.

question: Was the projection memo OPS-PROJ-04-29 a binding lift schedule for Service Zone 2?
reference_answer: No. OPS-PROJ-04-29 was an internal projection circulated 2026-04-29 16:00 and was conditional on no further positive samples; it was not a binding announcement, schedule, or commitment, and the packet explicitly states it should not be cited as one.

question: What event caused the projection in OPS-PROJ-04-29 to be superseded, and on what date and time did that event occur?
reference_answer: The second positive sample (event E-06) on 2026-04-30 09:00 superseded the projection by triggering a §6.2 clear-sample-clock reset.

question: Under which rule does the corrected timestamp on E-04 replace the as-logged timestamp for rule computations?
reference_answer: §6.5 (Timestamp correction).

question: Under which rule does the clear-sample clock pause when the sampler is offline?
reference_answer: §6.2a (Clock pause on sampler offline).

question: Under which rule did the §6.3 deadline shift from Sunday 2026-05-03 to Monday 2026-05-04?
reference_answer: §6.3 (specifically the weekend/holiday clause that shifts the deadline to close of business 17:00 on the next business day).

question: What is the status, as of packet close, of the engineering review report on the cause of the 2026-04-28 contamination, and by what date is it expected?
reference_answer: Not yet issued as of packet close on 2026-05-05; expected by 2026-05-19. Preliminary attribution is a main-line break upstream of Station S-3, but final attribution awaits the written engineering review report and may revise the cause.

question: What is the status, as of packet close, of the procedure review on whether OPS-PROJ-04-29 should have been suppressed, and on what date is the review scheduled?
reference_answer: Scheduled for 2026-05-13; the review's findings have not been issued as of packet close.

question: Did the second sampler-offline interval (maintenance, E-08 to E-09) trigger a §6.2 clear-sample reset?
reference_answer: No. Under §6.2a a sampler-offline pause does not constitute a positive sample and does not trigger §6.2; only the actual positive sample at E-06 on 2026-04-30 09:00 triggered the reset.

question: Was a separate Lift Notice issued to lift the Restricted-Use Notice in Service Zone 3?
reference_answer: No. The RUN closure on 2026-05-04 13:00 is recorded under E-13 and is not a separate Lift Notice; LFT-2026-05-04 is the only Lift Notice issued and applies to the BWN in Zone 2.

question: If the §6.3 weekend-shift rule had not been applied, by how many hours would the Authority have missed the §6.3 deadline given that the Lift Notice was issued at 2026-05-04 12:00?
reference_answer: 19 hours (the unshifted deadline would have been 2026-05-03 17:00 and the lift was issued 2026-05-04 12:00; this is the figure stated in §4).
